data.title: new-workspace should accept --title inline (currently requires a follow-up workspace-action rename)

data.body:
Captured 2026-05-19 while spawning the Substrate Phase 1 orchestrator workspace.

**Current behavior:**
- `c11 new-workspace --cwd <path>` creates a workspace named 'Workspace N' (the index).
- `--title` is not a flag on `new-workspace` (errors with: 'unknown flag --title. Known flags: --command, --cwd, --layout').
- The actual rename path is `c11 workspace-action --action rename --workspace <ref> --title <text>` — discoverable but not obvious.

**Proposed:**
Add `--title <text>` to `c11 new-workspace`. One command, named workspace, done.

```bash
c11 new-workspace --cwd /path --title 'Substrate'
```

**Operator quote:** 'workspace didn't get named. Maybe the new workspace command should allow the name to travel in line, so it's just one command to create the new workspace.'

Small, isolated change to the new-workspace command + plumbing through to whatever workspace-action rename calls. Both Layout-applied workspaces (via --layout) and bare workspaces should accept the same flag.

Tagging as medium — quality-of-life for operators (and orchestrator agents) creating workspaces from CLI.

data.body:
PR open: https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/225

`c11 new-workspace --title <text>` implemented. Title flows through workspace.create -> TabManager.setCustomTitle (same seam as workspace.rename), so it's the durable customTitle. Logic tests in NewWorkspaceTitleTests (c11LogicTests). Skill (SKILL.md + api.md) updated.

CI: all code gates green (build/logic-tests, compat-tests, mailbox-unit, remote-daemon-tests, drawbridge gate/judge/review). Only drawbridge-notify is red — Zulip webhook host unresolvable from the runner, infra-only, not a code gate. Not merged.
